serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of Mango Pomelo Sago is approximately 300g, served chilled in a bowl at a Chinese dessert shop or Cantonese restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Mango Pomelo Sago — HPB Singapore + USDA SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: HPB Singapore + USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient data adapted from HPB Singapore (foodo.sg) and USDA SR Legacy for the individual components of this dish (mango, sago, pomelo, coconut milk, evaporated milk). Values represent a weighted composite for the standard 300g bowl preparation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Mango and Sago Components — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 55 estimated from component foods: ripe mango GI ~55 (University of Sydney GI Database), sago pearls GI ~66 (University of Sydney GI Database). The fat content from coconut milk moderates the overall glycemic response. Composite GI of 55 is a weighted estimate placing this dish at the upper boundary of the low GI category (GI ≤55).

hypertension.guidance: With approximately 90mg of sodium per 300g serving, Mango Pomelo Sago is very low in sodium and is generally suitable for individuals managing hypertension; however, the 30g sugar and 2.7g saturated fat per serving should be considered in the context of overall cardiovascular risk management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Glycemic Index and total sugar content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of ~55 (low category) and a glycemic load of ~15 per 300g serving, Mango Pomelo Sago is a moderate glycemic dessert; the fructose from mango and fat from coconut milk help moderate the glycemic response, but individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size due to 30g total sugar per serving.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dessert contains 2.7g saturated fat and 30g sugar per 300g serving, both modest amounts; individuals with cardiovascular disease may consume this occasionally as part of a balanced diet, noting that the lauric acid in coconut milk has a mixed lipid profile effect.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mango Pomelo Sago (楊枝甘露, Yang Zhi Gan Lu) is a modern classic Cantonese cold dessert originating from Hong Kong in the 1980s, invented at Lei Garden restaurant by Chef Wong Weng Cheh. It features a rich, chilled base of pureed fresh mangoes blended with coconut milk and evaporated milk for a creamy, smooth texture. The dessert is defined by three distinct elements: the velvety mango puree, translucent sago (tapioca) pearls, and juicy, slightly tart pomelo segments that provide a refreshing citrus burst. A typical 300g bowl contains approximately 240 kcal, 2g protein, 4g fat, and 30g sugar, with a low-to-moderate GI of ~55 and GL of ~15. It is very low in sodium (~90mg per serving) and provides Vitamin C (~39mg) and beta-carotene from mango. CLINICAL ALERT: Pomelo (Citrus grandis) contains furanocoumarins that inhibit CYP3A4 enzymes, sharing the same drug interaction profile as grapefruit. The standard ~40g pomelo per bowl is likely below a clinically significant threshold, but patients on CYP3A4-sensitive medications (statins, immunosuppressants, amlodipine, cyclosporine) should be aware and consult their healthcare provider. This dessert is widely available at Chinese dessert shops in Singapore (e.g., Ah Chew Desserts, Honeymoon Desserts) and is considered one of the better glycemic choices among popular Singaporean desserts. Source: HPB Singapore + USDA SR Legacy.
common_user_queries: mango pomelo sago GI glycemic index

confidence_reason: Nutrient data is a composite adapted from HPB Singapore (foodo.sg) and USDA SR Legacy for the individual components of this mixed dessert. GI is an analogous estimate based on component foods from the University of Sydney GI Database. No single approved-source entry exists for this exact dish.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this mixed coconut milk and mango dessert is not reported as a composite dish in HPB Singapore (foodo.sg) or USDA SR Legacy as of 2026-04-14; only total fat and saturated fat are available. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this mixed chilled dessert is not reported in HPB Singapore or USDA SR Legacy for this composite preparation as of 2026-04-14. trans_fat_g confirmed 0.0 per USDA SR Legacy component data. vitamin_d_mcg confirmed 0.0 as this dessert contains no significant vitamin D sources. CLINICAL NOTE: Pomelo (Citrus grandis) inhibits CYP3A4 — same drug interaction profile as grapefruit; standard ~40g pomelo per bowl is likely below clinically significant threshold but patients on CYP3A4-sensitive medications should be aware. GI of 55 places this dish at the upper boundary of the low GI category (GI ≤55); gi_category is set to 'low' accordingly.
